what_it_does: A static HTML/CSS desktop mockup of the A3 view: an operator has selected an llm process node on the canvas, and the right-hand Inspector panel renders that node's real config form, its latest output, and actions such as force rerun.
observations: Uses ../design-system.css and an app-shell grid with top, left rail, canvas, footer, right panel, and a hidden mobile tabbar.
The left rail is a node palette grouped into the two live kinds: Process (11) and Content (5), with llm highlighted as selected.
The canvas shows a content constant node feeding a selected llm process node, plus a visual 'selected → inspector' tie.
The right panel has tabs: Inspector (active), Inbox (badge 2), and RHM.
The Inspector form maps to the real llm config_schema: model enum select, system textarea, temperature/top_p/max_tokens number inputs, base_url string input, and retries number input.
Model dropdown defaults to deepseek-v4-pro:cloud and lists other live chat models; a comment notes the list is fed by the registry and that Gemini is excluded.
Node status is shown as ran, and comments restrict status dots to idle/ran/cached/stuck, calling 'await' an inbox/presence state only.
Output block shows a sample memo-gate explanation with address run://demo/llm and status ran.
An 'Act on this output' row offers ↻ rerun (force), → inbox, and ⊕ build.
Extensive HTML comments document the journey moment, feature inventory references, backend code line references, and deliberate design choices (e.g., why llm was chosen over ask).
standouts: The Inspector is explicitly built from the selected node's config_schema, making it the single place config lives.
Every control is grounded with backend code references and inventory line numbers in comments.
The force rerun affordance is mapped to scheduler force=[ids] semantics.
Status vocabulary is strictly limited to idle/ran/cached/stuck.
The model dropdown is labeled as sourced from the live chat model fleet.
conventions: Follows the shared design-system token convention (CSS custom properties for color, spacing, radii).
Uses data-ui-ref attributes throughout for UI reference mapping.
Uses semantic sectioning (header, nav, main, aside, footer).
Employs compact, utility-style CSS classes for layout and form controls.
Includes responsive affordance for mobile via a tabbar that is hidden on desktop.
concerns: The model dropdown options are hardcoded in HTML even though the comment says they come from a live registry.
Some node/card classes (.bar, .body, .out, .cfg, .pill, .addr, .port) are used but not defined in this file; they likely rely on design-system.css.
Number bounds are described as conventional judgment calls rather than code-derived.
It is a static mockup with no visible interactivity or JavaScript.
notes: A heavily annotated desktop UI reference that ties every inspector control for a selected llm node back to real backend config fields and status semantics.
questions: How are the data-ui-ref URIs consumed by the design or engineering system?
Is the model select meant to be hydrated dynamically from /api/models?kind=chat, or is the hardcoded list only illustrative?
What does the 'RHM' tab stand for and what content belongs there?
Are the node/card styles (.bar, .body, .port, etc.) defined in design-system.css or another file?
Is the mobile tabbar functional or only a layout placeholder?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it is a desktop mockup of the node-selected inspector showing real config, output, and actions.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth for per-node config in the inspector.
Grounded design: every UI element must map to real backend fields and states.
Memoization and force-run as first-class runtime concepts.
Visual continuity between canvas selection and inspector context.
intention_signals: The file appears to be a high-fidelity, annotated reference intended to align designers and developers on exactly what controls, states, and actions belong in the inspector for a selected llm node, while preventing invented config or status values.
novelty: The extensive 'no fiction' inline commentary that links each UI element to backend code references, inventory lines, and explicit exclusions (e.g., no Gemini, no ask-only fields) is unusual for a static mockup.
contribution: A concrete, annotated desktop inspector mockup that documents the real config fields, control types, status states, and action affordances for a selected llm node, serving as an alignment artifact for UI/UX and implementation.
summary_for_embedding: A static HTML/CSS desktop mockup of the A3 inspector view for a selected llm process node, showing its config form, output, status, and action buttons with extensive grounding comments.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a grounded, high-fidelity reference for the desktop inspector UI when an operator selects a node to configure or re-run it.
